scheme_name: Gadiya Lohar Maharana Pratap Bhawan Nirman Yojana
page_title: Gadiya Lohar Maharana Pratap Bhawan Nirman Yojana

details: The scheme "Gadiya Lohar Maharana Pratap Bhawan Nirman Yojana" was introduced by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Rajasthan. Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹1,20,000/- is provided in three instalments to Gadiya Lohar families in Rajasthan for the construction of permanent houses and ensuring their settlement.
To provide permanent houses to the Gadiya Lohar families.
To provide financial assistance in three installments for house construction.
To promote the social and economic integration of Gadiya Lohars community.
benefits: ₹1,20,000/- is provided to each eligible beneficiary.
First Installment: ₹40,000/- is provided after verification of land ownership and submission of required documents.
Second Installment: ₹40,000/- is disbursed after verification of construction progress.
Third Installment: ₹40,000/- is given upon completion of construction and submission of utilization proof.
This is a one-time financial grant.
The entire house construction must be completed within one year from the approval of the first installment.
If a beneficiary misuses the grant, legal action will be taken, and the amount will be recovered.
The constructed house cannot be sold or transferred for 20 years.
eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
The applicant should belong to the Gadiya Lohar community.
The applicant should own a plot for house construction.
The applicant or their family should not own any other house within the district or anywhere in Rajasthan.

offline: The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the District Social Welfare Office and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the District Social Welfare Office.

Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the District Social Welfare Office to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission and a unique identification number (if applicable).

documents_required: Caste Certificate (Issued by a competent authority)
The applicant must declare on a ₹10 non-judicial stamp paper that they do not own any other house in the district or state and the house will not be sold or transferred for 20 years.
Construction Progress Report (for second and third installments)

faqs.question: Who can apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Individuals belonging to the Gadiya Lohar community who own a plot can apply.

faqs.question: How is the amount disbursed?
faqs.answer: The grant is provided in three installments based on the progress of house construction.

faqs.question: How often can I receive this grant?
faqs.answer: It is a one-time financial assistance for house construction.

faqs.question: What happens if I misuse the grant?
faqs.answer: The amount will be recovered, and legal action may be taken against the beneficiary.

faqs.question: Is there a deadline to complete the house construction?
faqs.answer: Yes, the house must be completed within one year from the approval of the first installment.

faqs.question: What is the selection process for this scheme?
faqs.answer: A committee consisting of district officials reviews applications and determines eligibility.

faqs.question: Can I apply online?
faqs.answer: No, applications must be submitted physically at the District Social Welfare Office.

faqs.question: What is the role of the District Social Welfare Officer in this scheme?
faqs.answer: The officer reviews applications, approves grants, and ensures proper utilization.

faqs.question: Can I sell or transfer the house after receiving the grant?
faqs.answer: No, the house cannot be sold, mortgaged, or transferred for 20 years.

faqs.question: How can I apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant needs to fill out the application form and submit it to the District Social Welfare Officer along with the required documents.
